name: Guardrail Bridge
description: Pre-agent guardrail-bridge plugin with blacklist and HTTP connectors.

http.apiKey.label: Guardrail HTTP API Key
http.apiKey.help: Use an OpenClaw SecretRef when possible; plain string values are supported but not recommended. SecretRef values are resolved at request time, not during plugin registration.

channels.*.http.apiKey.label: Channel Guardrail HTTP API Key
channels.*.http.apiKey.help: Channel-specific OpenClaw SecretRef or plain string API key. SecretRef values are resolved at request time. Channel keys do not inherit across provider/apiUrl retargets unless explicitly configured.

connector.description: Connector type for safety checks. Empty string or omitted = auto-detect from http/blacklist fields. Global connector is optional — channels can independently enable Guardrails.

provider.description: HTTP provider name. Built-in: 'dknownai', 'secra', 'hidylan'. Built-in apiKey behavior: 'dknownai' and 'secra' require apiKey; 'hidylan' currently treats apiKey as optional. Custom providers can be registered via registerHttpProvider().

apiKey.description: Provider API key as a plain string or OpenClaw SecretRef. SecretRef values are resolved at request time inside checks, not during plugin registration or provider init. Required by built-in 'dknownai' and 'secra'; optional for built-in 'hidylan'. Plain strings are supported but not recommended.

apiUrl.description: Endpoint URL. Optional override for built-in providers.

model.description: Model version. Current built-in 'dknownai', 'secra', and 'hidylan' ignore it; custom providers may use it.

blockMessage.default: This request has been blocked by the guardrail-bridge policy.

blacklistFile.description: Keyword file source. true = default path ~/.openclaw/guardrail-bridge/keywords.txt; string = custom path; false = disabled.

hot.description: Reload keywords file automatically when it changes

hotDebounceMs.description: Debounce interval for hot-reload in ms

channels.description: Per-channel configuration overrides. Keys are channel IDs. Each channel can independently enable Guardrails even when no global connector is configured.

connector.description: Override or independently enable a connector for this channel
